channel.title: Billing on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Billing on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: AI credit pools for cost centers in the billing UI
item.description:
GitHub Billing UI now lets you manage a cost center’s AI credit pool directly when creating or editing cost centers. Before this change, that configuration was only available through another interface.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot users can now see AI credits used per billing cycle
item.description:
Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise users can now view how many AI credits they have used in the current billing cycle, even if they do not have an individual budget. The usage count is available in GitHub Copilot usage.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Per-user budgets for cost centers in the billing UI
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Cloud now supports creating per-user budgets for cost centers directly in the billing UI. Enterprise admins can manage these user-level budgets alongside cost centers and budgets in one place.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Billing Preview app will be retired on August 3
item.description:
GitHub will retire the Copilot Billing Preview app on August 3, 2026. Users who rely on it for Copilot spend review are directed to use built-in GitHub visibility instead.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Cost centers now support enterprise teams
item.description:
GitHub cost centers now support adding enterprise teams as resources, so usage can be attributed according to your team structure. This update extends cost attribution for enterprises beyond individual resources.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: AI credits consumed per user now in the Copilot usage metrics API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API now includes per-user AI credits consumed per day. The new data is derived from the same AI credits consumption source used by the usage-based billing API.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Managed identity for Foundry billing calls from Azure AI Search
item.description:
Azure AI Search now supports managed identity authentication for billing operations against Microsoft Foundry resources instead of the previous key-based flow. Customers can use either a system-assigned or user-assigned identity on the search service.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Updates to GitHub Copilot billing and plans
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now uses usage-based billing for all users. Copilot code review also consumes GitHub Actions minutes, in addition to GitHub billing.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Hard budget limits now available for GitHub Advanced Security
item.description:
GitHub Advanced Security now supports hard budget limits for GHAS SKUs, allowing enterprise admins and billing managers to prevent teams from exceeding allocated license budgets. This applies to license-based GHAS products.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Auto activation for SaaS subscriptions in Microsoft Marketplace
item.description:
Auto activation for SaaS subscriptions is now generally available in Microsoft Marketplace. When enabled, the subscription and billing cycle start as soon as the purchase flow is completed.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: April reports are now available to prepare for usage-based billing
item.description:
GitHub now lets customers download April usage reports to see how Copilot activity maps to AI credits ahead of the new usage-based billing rollout. The billing unit of AI credits is going live on a staged schedule.

  • Source: GitHub
